key_insight: DeFAb turns defeasible abduction into a fully verifiable benchmark, showing that symbolic reasoning is dramatically more reliable than frontier language models on theory revision tasks.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark offers a fast, exact verifier that can serve both as an evaluation standard and as a reward signal for training methods like DPO, RLVR, and GRPO.
method: Generate instances from large public knowledge bases and property graphs using defeasible logic rules.
Require each hypothesis to satisfy polynomial-time checks for derivation, conservativity, and minimality.
Provide multi-level datasets plus harder variants (DeFAb-Hard, CONJURE) with kernel-verified gold standards.
evidence_signal: Across 372,648+ instances from 18 sources, the symbolic solver achieved 100% accuracy in under 50 microseconds, while the best frontier model peaked at 65% and fell to 23.5% under rendering-robust evaluation.
watch_item: Performance claims depend on exact surface rendering and benchmark construction, so care is needed when transferring results to open-ended real-world reasoning tasks.
paper_title: DeFAb: A Verifiable Benchmark for Defeasible Abduction in Foundation Models
